analysis: The hybrid CP-PSO approach (Agent 1) achieved the best score (8349), significantly outperforming pure CP (Agents 0 and 6, score 10738). However, several agents failed due to feasibility issues or timeouts. The CP-PSO hybrid shows promise but may benefit from further refinement. Exploring alternative metaheuristics or hybrid approaches could yield further improvements. The pure CP approaches are stable but less effective on harder instances, suggesting room for enhancement through better search strategies or hybridization.

assignments.direction: Refine the hybrid CP-PSO approach by optimizing the balance between CP and PSO phases, focusing on adaptive time allocation and improved solution integration between the two methods.
Enhance the CP-PSO hybrid by incorporating local search techniques within the PSO phase to refine particle positions and improve convergence speed, particularly for larger instances.
Combine the CP-PSO hybrid with genetic algorithm components, using CP for feasibility checks and PSO/GA for population-based exploration, to leverage the strengths of all three approaches.

Improve the constraint programming model by integrating the redundant constraints and symmetry-breaking techniques from Agent 6 into the CP-PSO hybrid to further reduce the search space and enhance solution quality.
Investigate a hybrid approach combining constraint programming with ant colony optimization, where CP ensures feasibility and ACO explores the solution space through pheromone-based search.
Explore a multi-start local search strategy that initializes solutions using constraint programming and refines them through iterative local search with adaptive neighborhood structures.
Develop a hybrid approach combining constraint programming with a deep reinforcement learning agent that learns to guide the CP solver's search strategy based on instance features.
Investigate a portfolio approach that dynamically selects between constraint programming, PSO, and genetic algorithms based on real-time performance feedback during the solving process.
